twitter_thread: 63% of households can't afford a $1k emergency. What's the real solution?
Cancel Netflix, gym memberships, and music streaming: $150/mo saved
50% of $4k/mo income goes to necessities, 30% to discretionary spending
Lifestyle inflation costs $10k/yr for every $20k raise: avoid the trap
Buy refurbished iPhones: $500 vs $1k new, 50% savings
Mint tracks $1.5m in expenses daily: what's your expense ratio?
What's the most effective way you've found to reduce expenses and build savings? #affordabilitycrisis #personalfinance
pack.linkedin: 63% of households can't afford a $1k emergency. Cutting subscription services like Netflix and gym memberships can save $150/mo. Allocating 50% of a $4k/mo income to necessities and 30% to discretionary spending also helps. Avoiding lifestyle inflation, which can cost $10k/yr for every $20k raise, is crucial. Buying refurbished items like iPhones can save up to 50%. Tools like Mint track $1.5m in expenses daily. What strategies have you found most effective in reducing expenses and building savings?
tiktok_hooks: 63 percent can't afford a thousand bucks
cut your subscription bills in half
pack.reddit_headline: Is the Affordability Crisis a Result of Poor Financial Planning or a Broader Economic Issue?
